children.text: not less than 85 percent for distribution under section 2351 or 2352 of this title, of which not more than 15 percent of the 85 percent may be used in accordance with subsection (c);

children.label: A
children.text: an amount equal to not more than 2 percent of the amount allotted to the State under for the fiscal year shall be made available to serve individuals in State institutions, such as State correctional institutions, juvenile justice facilities, and educational institutions that serve individuals with disabilities;section 2321 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: not less than $60,000 and not more than $150,000 shall be available for services that prepare individuals for non-traditional fields; and

children.label: i
children.text: an amount equal to 0.1 percent; or

children.label: D
children.text: assuring compliance with all applicable Federal laws;

children.label: F
children.text: supporting and developing State data systems relevant to the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: b Matching requirement
children.text: Each eligible agency receiving funds made available under subsection (a)(3) shall match, from non-Federal sources and on a dollar-for-dollar basis, the funds received under subsection (a)(3).

children.label: B
children.text: areas with high percentages of CTE concentrators or CTE participants;

children.label: C
children.text: areas with high numbers of CTE concentrators or CTE participants; and

children.label: D
children.text: areas with disparities or gaps in performance as described in ; andsection 2323(b)(3)(C)(ii)(II) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: foster innovation through the identification and promotion of promising and proven career and technical education programs, practices, and strategies, which may include programs, practices, and strategies that prepare individuals for nontraditional fields; or

children.label: B
children.text: promote the development, implementation, and adoption of programs of study or career pathways aligned with State-identified high-skill, high-wage, or in-demand occupations or industries.
